title: Improve OpenAI model name matching for max_completion_tokens
body:
## Context

From PR #223 code review (fingerprint: `69ab5e5ec2d89cbeed7a8d83f7c86730`)

## Problem

The `usesMaxCompletionTokens` function in `internal/adapter/llm/simple/openai.go` uses string prefix matching which could incorrectly classify models. For example, a hypothetical model named `gpt-50` would match the `gpt-5` prefix check but might not support `max_completion_tokens`.

## Current Implementation

```go
newPrefixes := []string{"gpt-5", "gpt-6", "gpt-7", "gpt-8", "gpt-9"}
for _, prefix := range newPrefixes {
    if strings.HasPrefix(modelLower, prefix) {
        return true
    }
}
```

## Suggested Improvements

1. Use exact model name matching or version parsing
2. Maintain a whitelist of models that support `max_completion_tokens`
3. Parse version numbers properly (e.g., extract major version)

## Priority

Low - current implementation works for all known models. Future-proofing measure.

## Related

- PR #223: Theme extraction feature
